name: Embalse de la Tejera and Castillo de Torresaviñán — circular from Cifuentes
name_original: Embalse de la Tejera y Castillo de Torresaviñán — circular desde Cifuentes
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cifuentes traverse a landscape characterized by the Alto Tajo Natural Park, offering varied terrain with significant elevation changes. The region features rolling hills, river valleys, and agricultural areas, including notable lavender fields. Reservoirs and historical structures like castles are also present, providing diverse scenery for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Cifuentes The most popular road cycling route is Alto Tajo Natural Park Loop, a 68.6 miles (110.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbing through the natural park. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Alcarria Lavender Fields Trail from Cifuentes, a difficult 52.0 miles (83.7 km) path. This route offers views of the agricultural landscape, including lavender fields, with over 3 hours 53 minutes of riding. Local road cyclists also love the Embalse de la Tejera and Castillo de Torresaviñán — circular from Cifuentes, a 44.5 miles (71.6 km) trail leading through reservoir areas and past historical sites, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Road cycling around Cifuentes is defined by natural park landscapes, reservoir views, and agricultural fields. The network offers options for moderate to challenging ability levels. The routes in Cifuentes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 290 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cifuentes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Cifuentes?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Cifuentes, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes more than 290 times, contributing to an average rating of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Cifuentes?
A: The terrain around Cifuentes is diverse, characterized by the Alto Tajo Natural Park. You'll encounter rolling hills, significant elevation changes, river valleys, and agricultural areas. Routes often feature views of reservoirs and historical structures like castles, providing varied scenery.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes with significant elevation gain in Cifuentes?
A: Yes, Cifuentes offers several challenging road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1020954201/alto-tajo-natural-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Tajo Natural Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 68.6-mile (110.4 km) trail with over 1,550 meters of elevation gain, taking approximately 5 hours and 18 minutes to complete. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Viaduct – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 120 km with over 1,690 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any moderate road cycling routes suitable for intermediate riders?
A: Absolutely. Cifuentes has 12 moderate road cycling routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018774342/embalse-de-la-tejera-and-castillo-de-torresavinan-circular-from-cifuentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Tejera and Castillo de Torresaviñán — circular from Cifuentes&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.5-mile (71.6 km) trail that leads through reservoir areas and past historical sites, typically completed in about 3 hours and 36 minutes. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes – Brihuega lavender fields loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 59.3 km route with over 580 meters of elevation gain.

Q: What can I see along the road cycling routes in Cifuentes?
A: Road cycling routes in Cifuentes offer diverse sights. You can pass by the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5902743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, explore the agricultural landscape with its notable lavender fields, or cycle past historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414485" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Arbeteta Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7249960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Ocentejo Castle&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also feature scenic views of reservoirs.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Cifuentes?
A: Yes, several circular road cycling routes start from Cifuentes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018774342/embalse-de-la-tejera-and-castillo-de-torresavinan-circular-from-cifuentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Tejera and Castillo de Torresaviñán — circular from Cifuentes&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 44.5-mile loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes – Brihuega lavender fields loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 59.3 km circular route.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Cifuentes?
A: The region's climate makes spring and early summer particularly appealing for road cycling, especially to witness the lavender fields in bloom. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be hot, so early morning rides are recommended during this season.

Q: Are there routes that showcase the lavender fields around Cifuentes?
A: Yes, you can experience the famous lavender fields on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018745352/la-alcarria-lavender-fields-trail-from-cifuentes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Alcarria Lavender Fields Trail from Cifuentes&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 52.0-mile (83.7 km) path offers extensive views of the agricultural landscape, including the vibrant lavender fields, and takes approximately 3 hours and 53 minutes to complete.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Cifuentes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse natural park landscapes, the scenic reservoir views, and the agricultural fields, particularly the lavender fields. The variety of routes, from moderate to challenging, is also a frequently highlighted aspect.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique geological features to see while cycling?
A: Yes, the Cifuentes area is home to several natural monuments. You can find the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5902743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cifuentes River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6689105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tetas de Viana (1,145 m)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5610581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Arco&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443557" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cívica Rock Dwellings and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; also offer a unique blend of natural beauty and historical interest.

Q: What is the longest road cycling route available in the Cifuentes area?
A: One of the longest routes in the area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Entrepeñas Viaduct – Entrepeñas Reservoir Dam loop from Central Nuclear de Trillo&lt;/a&gt;. This challenging route spans approximately 119.3 km (74.1 miles) and features over 1,690 meters of elevation gain, typically requiring around 6 hours and 9 minutes to complete.
tips: Torrecuadrada de los Valles is a Spanish town in the northeast of the province of Guadalajara, belonging to the municipality of Torremocha del Campo with a population of 35 inhabitants. The term of the municipality, in the foothills of the Iberian System in the Sierra del Ducado.

The Torresaviñán castle, from the 12th century, is located on a hill very close to Torremocha del Campo and is visible from the Barcelona highway itself. It is of Muslim origin and due to the absence of amenities in its structure, it is assumed that it was a defensive post, rather than a permanent settlement. Its current state is due to the blowing up of its walls by the Austrian army during the war of succession in the 18th century.

Moranchel is a small hamlet in the Alcarreña town of Cifuentes. It is located on the route of the Ruta de la Lana and has a curious historic center that is currently decorated with murals on the houses.
